retry_reason: Failure means no next device exists at this moment; retrying with same parameters will likely return NULL again unless the bus topology changes dynamically, which is not part of the wrapper's contract.
contract_summary: This is a get-wrapper that finds the next device on the bus after a given device and returns it with an incremented reference count. The caller must release the reference with put_device() when done. It is typically used in iterators: start with cur=NULL and call repeatedly until NULL is returned. The caller must check the return value and not use the device after releasing the reference.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to find the next device on the bus and acquire a reference on it via bus_find_device. No other logic exists.

check.reason: bus_find_next_device returns the result of bus_find_device, which acquires a reference on the device. The callee info confirms a get operation on the return value. The access path dev.kobj.kref.refcount and type chain device--kobject--kref--refcount_t are consistent with struct device layout.
